---
private: true
emoji: "🧹"
name: Daily yamllint Fixer
description: Daily workflow that runs yamllint on generated *.lock.yml files, analyzes noise (trailing spaces, indentation), and opens a pull-request with Go generator fixes
on:
  schedule: daily on weekdays
  workflow_dispatch:
  skip-if-match: 'is:pr is:open label:yamllint-fixer in:title "[yamllint-fixer]"'
max-daily-ai-credits: 10000
permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: read
  issues: read
engine:
  id: claude
max-turns: 120
network:
  allowed:
    - defaults
    - go
    - python
tools:
  cli-proxy: true
  github:
    mode: gh-proxy
    toolsets: [default]
  bash:
    - "*"
  edit:
safe-outputs:
  create-pull-request:
    expires: 7d
    title-prefix: "[yamllint-fixer] "
    labels: [automation, yamllint-fixer]
    draft: true
  noop:
timeout-minutes: 30
strict: true
if: needs.yamllint_check.outputs.has_warnings == 'true'
jobs:
  yamllint_check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [activation]
    permissions:
      contents: read
    outputs:
      has_warnings: ${{ steps.run_yamllint.outputs.has_warnings }}
      warning_count: ${{ steps.run_yamllint.outputs.warning_count }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v7.0.0
        with:
          persist-credentials: false
      - name: Build gh-aw from source
        run: |
          set -e
          cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
          make build
          echo "gh-aw version: $("$GITHUB_WORKSPACE/gh-aw" --version)"
      - name: Install yamllint
        run: |
          set -e
          pip3 install yamllint==1.38.0 --quiet
          yamllint --version
      - name: Run yamllint on generated lock files
        id: run_yamllint
        run: |
          set -e
          YAMLLINT_CONFIG='{extends: default, rules: {line-length: disable, document-start: disable, truthy: {check-keys: false}, comments: {require-starting-space: true, min-spaces-from-content: 1}}}'
          mkdir -p /tmp/gh-aw/agent/yamllint
          yamllint -d "$YAMLLINT_CONFIG" -f parsable \
            "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"/.github/workflows/*.lock.yml 2>&1 \
            | tee /tmp/gh-aw/agent/yamllint/warnings-before.txt || true
          WARNING_COUNT=$(wc -l < /tmp/gh-aw/agent/yamllint/warnings-before.txt)
          echo "Total warnings/errors: $WARNING_COUNT"
          echo "warning_count=$WARNING_COUNT" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          if [ "$WARNING_COUNT" -gt 0 ]; then
            echo "has_warnings=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "has_warnings=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi
      - name: Upload yamllint results artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v7.0.1
        with:
          name: yamllint-results
          path: /tmp/gh-aw/agent/yamllint/warnings-before.txt
          if-no-files-found: error
          retention-days: 3
steps:
  - name: Download yamllint results artifact
    uses: actions/download-artifact@v8.0.1
    with:
      name: yamllint-results
      path: /tmp/gh-aw/agent/yamllint
  - name: Install yamllint
    run: |
      set -e
      pip3 install yamllint==1.38.0 --quiet
      yamllint --version
imports:
  - shared/otlp.md
sandbox:
  agent:
    sudo: false
evals:
  - id: lint_issues_identified
    question: Does the agent output confirm that yamllint issues were identified and analyzed in the generated lock files?
  - id: pr_created_or_noop
    question: Was a pull request created with Go generator fixes for yamllint issues, or was noop used when no issues required changes?
---

# Daily yamllint Fixer

You are a Go code quality agent. Your mission is to reduce yamllint noise in the
`*.lock.yml` files generated by gh-aw, by fixing the root causes in the Go generator code.

## Context

- **Repository**: `${{ github.repository }}`
- **Workspace**: `${{ github.workspace }}`
- **yamllint config**: `{extends: default, rules: {line-length: disable, document-start: disable, truthy: {check-keys: false}, comments: {require-starting-space: true, min-spaces-from-content: 1}}}`

The `yamllint_check` job has already:
1. Built `gh-aw` from source
2. Run yamllint 1.38.0 and saved a baseline report to `/tmp/gh-aw/agent/yamllint/warnings-before.txt`

The agent job pre-steps have:
- Downloaded the baseline artifact to `/tmp/gh-aw/agent/yamllint/warnings-before.txt`
- Installed yamllint 1.38.0 (available on `PATH`)

The agent still needs to build `gh-aw` from source before Phases 3–5 (use `make build` in the workspace).

## Phase 1: Analyze Baseline

Read `/tmp/gh-aw/agent/yamllint/warnings-before.txt` and categorize each warning type:

```bash
cat /tmp/gh-aw/agent/yamllint/warnings-before.txt | grep -oP '\[.*?\]' | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
```

Focus on the most frequent categories:
- `[trailing-spaces]` — lines with trailing whitespace
- `[indentation]` — wrong indentation level
- `[comments-indentation]` — comment not indented like its content
- `[empty-lines]` — too many consecutive blank lines

For each category, sample a few affected lines:

```bash
grep "trailing-spaces" /tmp/gh-aw/agent/yamllint/warnings-before.txt | head -5
```

Then inspect those lines in the lock file to understand what generates them.

## Phase 2: Trace Root Causes

The main generator code lives in:
- `pkg/workflow/header.go` — produces the file header with ASCII logo
- `pkg/workflow/assets/logo.txt` — the ASCII art logo (lines with trailing spaces)
- `pkg/workflow/frontmatter_extraction_yaml.go` — `indentYAMLLines()` and YAML extraction
- `pkg/workflow/compiler_yaml.go` — main YAML compilation

Common root causes to investigate:

### 2a. Trailing spaces from ASCII logo

Open `pkg/workflow/assets/logo.txt` and check:
```bash
cat -A pkg/workflow/assets/logo.txt | head -20
```

If logo lines have trailing spaces (`$` appears after spaces), `header.go` generates comment
lines like `# logo-line   ` (trailing spaces). Fix options:
1. Trim trailing whitespace from each logo line when writing in `header.go`
2. Strip trailing spaces from `logo.txt` directly (but this may break alignment tests — check
   `pkg/workflow/compiler_yaml_logo_test.go` before doing this)

Prefer option 1: in `header.go`, change the logo-line loop to strip trailing spaces:
```go
fmt.Fprintf(&header, "# %s\n", strings.TrimRight(line, " \t"))
```

After applying this change, run the logo-specific tests to confirm nothing broke:
```bash
cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE" && go test ./pkg/workflow/ -run TestLogo -v
```

### 2b. Trailing spaces in blank lines within multi-line strings

Multi-line YAML blocks (like heredoc shell scripts) can emit blank lines that carry the
surrounding indentation as pure-whitespace content. Look for the pattern in `indentYAMLLines()`
in `frontmatter_extraction_yaml.go`:

```go
} else {
    result.WriteString("\n" + lines[i])
}
```

When `lines[i]` is non-empty whitespace (e.g., `"          "`), this emits trailing spaces.
Fix: replace with `result.WriteString("\n")` for all-whitespace lines.

### 2c. Indentation under `schedule:`

If yamllint reports `[indentation]` warnings near cron schedule blocks, look at how
schedule items are serialized. The pattern:
```yaml
on:
  schedule:
  - cron: "..."
```
has the list item at 2-space indent instead of 4-space. Check the YAML marshaling options
in `pkg/workflow/yaml_options.go` and the schedule serialization.

## Phase 3: Apply Fixes

Apply only the fixes you can confirm by tracing the code. Be surgical:

1. Read the relevant Go files.
2. Apply the minimal change (using `edit` tool).
3. After each file edit, run `go build ./...` in the workspace to confirm it compiles.

After all edits, run `make fmt` to normalize formatting:
```bash
cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE" && make fmt
```

## Phase 4: Rebuild and Recompile

Rebuild the binary and recompile all workflows:

```bash
cd "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"
make build
"$GITHUB_WORKSPACE/gh-aw" compile 2>&1 | tee /tmp/gh-aw/agent/compile-output.txt || true
```

If compilation has errors (not yamllint-related), stop and document them — do not create a PR
that breaks existing workflows.

## Phase 5: Verify Improvement

Run yamllint again with the same config:

```bash
YAMLLINT_CONFIG='{extends: default, rules: {line-length: disable, document-start: disable, truthy: {check-keys: false}, comments: {require-starting-space: true, min-spaces-from-content: 1}}}'
yamllint -d "$YAMLLINT_CONFIG" -f parsable \
  "$GITHUB_WORKSPACE"/.github/workflows/*.lock.yml 2>&1 \
  | tee /tmp/gh-aw/agent/yamllint/warnings-after.txt || true

BEFORE=$(wc -l < /tmp/gh-aw/agent/yamllint/warnings-before.txt)
AFTER=$(wc -l < /tmp/gh-aw/agent/yamllint/warnings-after.txt)
echo "Before: $BEFORE warnings | After: $AFTER warnings | Reduction: $((BEFORE - AFTER))"
```

**Only proceed to create a PR if the warning count decreases.**

If the warning count did not decrease, call `noop` with an explanation.

## Phase 6: Create Pull Request

If the warning count decreased, commit the Go source changes and emit a `create_pull_request`
safe output.

**Do not stage `.github/workflows/*.lock.yml` changes** — only stage changes to `pkg/` files
(the generator source code). Reviewers will recompile the lock files after the Go changes are
merged.

PR body template:

```markdown
## Summary

Reduces yamllint noise in generated `*.lock.yml` files by fixing root causes in the Go
YAML generator code.

**yamllint config used**:
```yaml
{extends: default, rules: {line-length: disable, document-start: disable, truthy: {check-keys: false}, comments: {require-starting-space: true, min-spaces-from-content: 1}}}
```

## Results

| Metric | Before | After | Reduction |
|--------|--------|-------|-----------|
| Total warnings | {BEFORE} | {AFTER} | {REDUCTION} ({PCT}%) |

### Warning breakdown

| Category | Before | After |
|----------|--------|-------|
| trailing-spaces | ... | ... |
| indentation | ... | ... |
| comments-indentation | ... | ... |
| empty-lines | ... | ... |

## Changes

{Brief description of each Go file changed and why}

## Notes

- The generated `.lock.yml` files are not included in this PR.
  Run `gh aw compile` after merging to regenerate them with the improvements.
- Closes #41507
```

## Exit Rules

- **Always** end with either `create_pull_request` or `noop`.
- Call `noop` if:
  - The baseline yamllint output already has 0 warnings
  - Your fixes do not reduce the warning count
  - Compilation fails after your edits (revert changes first)
  - No root cause could be traced to Go generator code

### Output Format

Structure reports as: overview → key metrics/issues → collapsible detail → next actions.

Wrap long content with `<details><summary><b>View Details</b></summary>...</details>`.
